name: Mud Hen Water
tagline: Modern Hawaiian cuisine in Kaimuki that showcases native ingredients — Ed Kenney's love letter to the land.
mustOrder: Poi sourdough bread — tangy, purple-tinged, baked fresh daily
Kalo (taro) nachos — local twist on nachos with taro chips and kalua pork
Seasonal fish — always fresh, prepared with native Hawaiian flavors
parking: Street parking on Waialae Ave or side streets. Kaimuki parking can be tough on weekends — go early.
talkStory: Chef Ed Kenney opened Mud Hen Water in 2014 as a love letter to native Hawaiian ingredients — the name is the literal English translation of 'Waikiki' (wai=water, kiki=spouting, but the area was historically known for mud hen habitat). Kenney, also behind Town restaurant, was a James Beard semifinalist and advocates a 'local first, organic whenever possible' sourcing philosophy. The poi sourdough bread uses real poi from Waiahole Valley taro farmers. A key voice in Hawaii's food sovereignty movement.
